product.name: ArNeo Flame + Static Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Fluorescencyjne dzianiny hi-vis na odzież ostrzegawczą
product.familyDescriptorEn: Fluorescent hi-vis knits for safety wear
product.categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
product.titleDescriptor: Flame Retardant, Antistatic, High Visible, Safety Jacket Fabric
product.specLine: 150 g/m², 99% Polyester 1% Carbon, High Visible, Flame Retardant
descriptions: Antistatic Knit Fabric ArNeo Pro is 99% polyester 1% carbon, 150 g/m² warp knitted flame retardant, antistatic, neon colored fabric for safety jackets.
It is mostly used by aeroport apron workers also for oil and gas industries.
Neon dyed fabrics they attract more attention than other colors. They absorb uv light and reflect as a visible light.
As a result they shine more than other objects.
Flame retardant certified. (TS EN ISO 14116) (resistant to 10 washing)
Flame Retardant, Antistatic, High Visible, Safety Jacket Fabric
150 g/m², 99% Polyester 1% Carbon, High Visible, Flame Retardant
technicalParameters: Dimensional Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

evidence: applicationImageLabels: Flame Retardant Antistatic Safety Jacket EN 20471
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicitly certified and marketed as high-visibility warp knit fabric for safety jackets under EN ISO 20471.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Used by oil and gas industry workers and antistatic per EN 1149, but no explicit electrical-utility, arc-protection or high-voltage application stated.
Mentioned use by airport apron workers, but this is an occupational context, not an explicit vehicle/vessel transport application.

evidence: applicationImageLabels: Flame Retardant Antistatic Safety Jacket EN 20471
primary.rationale: Product is explicitly a certified high-visibility warp knit fabric for safety jackets under EN ISO 20471, positioned as the core commercial use.

secondary.rationale: Mentions of airport apron and oil & gas worker use are occupational context rather than an independently stated distinct application; no explicit transport, energy, or workwear-industrial end-product framing beyond the hi-vis safety jacket use.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Antistatic and used near oil/gas industries, but no explicit electrical-utility or arc-protection application stated; rule 8 excludes antistatic alone as basis.
Airport apron worker mention is occupational context, not an explicit vehicle/vessel transport application per rule 10.
General industrial use is implied but hivis is clearly the dominant and more specific certified application, superseding generic workwear classification.
